understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked with `+`, with the extra constraint that if there are multiple `+` tiles they must all be occupied by pieces at the same time for the engine to report WIN; completing all levels wins the whole game within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A piece can be selected and then moved via actions; CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked grid coordinate, but the move only succeeds if the destination is reachable/valid under the level’s constraints. When the destination is blocked (by walls/obstacles or an invalid/unreachable target), the piece’s position does not change but the step is still consumed. The logs suggest movement is discrete on grid coordinates, obstacles prevent movement, and efficient step use is required because running out of steps ends the attempt.

orient_history: MOVE actions attempt to shift the selected 3x3 piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but collisions with wall tiles block movement. CLICK likely changes selection to another selectable (or interacts with targets) to enable progress.

CLICK appears to place the currently selected piece at the clicked pixel position while leaving a selectable behind at the previous position (effectively duplicating). Winning likely requires occupying every + cell in the goal region simultaneously, so we should CLICK to place/selectables onto each + cell.

CLICK appears to attempt to place the selected 3x3 piece with its top-left at the target pixel position, succeeding if that placement does not collide with any wall; goals are non-collidable and should register WIN when a piece overlaps/occupies the goal area. The failed [33,15] placement suggests tight pixel-level wall boundaries around the goal region, so using the goal object's exact pixel anchor may fit better.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves by grid steps but we are boxed in by walls, so we likely must use CLICK to place/teleport the selected piece onto a valid tile (likely the goal region). CLICK may require grid-aligned pixel coordinates; the goal at [35,17] is not aligned to 3px cells, so clicking an aligned point inside the goal area might work.

Hypothesis: CLICK primarily changes selection when clicking a selectable; movement is grid-step (3px) but both pieces are trapped, so we likely need the correct piece selected before attempting any goal-interaction/teleport (if supported). Next: select the other piece to test whether subsequent clicks/moves behave differently.

Hypothesis: CLICK primarily changes which selectable is active (or toggles a carry/pickup state when clicking the selected piece). Since MOVE is impossible due to walls, the intended mechanic likely involves a CLICK-based pickup/place interaction to get a piece onto the goal.

CLICK seems to change selection when clicking another selectable; it may also attempt a direct move/teleport to the clicked coordinate if the rules allow (possibly along a row/column). Since both pieces are boxed in by walls, trying a CLICK directly onto a goal-aligned coordinate is the best next probe.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is currently controlled (appears like the 'player' position changes), while MOVE_* attempts to shift the selected piece by 3 pixels but is blocked by adjacent walls. Need to confirm selection-switch behavior by clicking the other selectable.

Hypothesis: CLICK may allow interacting with adjacent wall/tiles (possibly carving/creating space or a swap) even when MOVE is blocked; need to test interaction with a directly adjacent blocked cell to find an escape toward the goal area.

CLICK selects a selectable piece at the clicked position. MOVE_* attempts to slide the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by walls/obstacles. To reach the goal at [35,17], we may need to select the other piece and see if it has any available moves.

Hypothesis: CLICK can move the currently selected piece by swapping/teleporting specifically onto another selectable's position even through walls; generic clicks on empty/goal tiles have not worked so far. Next I should reposition onto the other selectable and re-test interactions from there.

CLICK appears to change which selectable is selected (not teleport). MOVE_* attempts to move the selected piece by 1 cell (3 px); currently the selected piece is enclosed by walls. Unsure yet if moving into walls can trigger another mechanic (pushing/breaking/toggling).

Likely mechanics: CLICK on a selectable changes the selected piece; movement/CLICK-to-move only works if there is open space (not immediately walled in). Current selected piece appears trapped, so we should switch selection to the other piece and try moving it toward the goal.

CLICK likely interacts with other selectable pieces (either swapping positions or switching control), since past CLICKs moved the selected piece between known selectable locations even when arrow movement was blocked. To make progress toward the goal, we should try interacting with the other selectable at [21,21].

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves 1 cell (3 px) but is blocked by collidable walls; CLICK attempts to move the selected selectable directly to the clicked coordinate if that destination is allowed (empty/goal) and not blocked by some rule (e.g., must be within reachable area). Goal tiles are non-collidable, so clicking the goal position may place the piece onto it and win.

CLICK appears to let us switch control to the selectable at the clicked position (even though the log phrases it as the selected piece 'moving'). Since the currently selected piece is immobile, we should select the other piece and see if it has a path or can be moved via subsequent clicks/moves toward the goal area.

CLICK appears to switch/select a piece (or interact with it) more reliably than moving from this trapped position. To make progress, I likely need to select the other selectable and check if it has any available movement routes toward the goal area.

CLICK attempts to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked pixel coordinate, but placement must be grid-aligned (multiples of 3) to fit without colliding with surrounding walls; MOVE actions are blocked in the current pocket.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* shifts by 3 pixels (one cell) but requires adjacent space; CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece to the clicked cell (likely only succeeds on valid empty/goal cell coordinates aligned to the 3-pixel grid). The goal '+' region likely corresponds to cells x=11-13,y=5-7, i.e., pixel-aligned top-lefts around [33,15]..[39,21].

CLICK appears to change which selectable is currently controlled (the 'player' becomes that piece). Arrow moves then attempt to move that selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell), with collisions against walls; reaching the goal likely requires maneuvering at least one piece into the goal region.

CLICK seems to relocate the currently selected piece to the clicked selectable/empty spot when valid (possibly swapping if occupied). MOVE_* then moves in 3-pixel (1-cell) steps when not blocked by walls. To reach the goal region, we likely must operate from the [21,21] side.

CLICK selects/switches the active selectable piece when clicking on its position. MOVE_* attempts to slide the currently selected 3x3 piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is blocked by collidable walls/objects. To win, we likely need to maneuver one/both selectables onto the goal tiles; since the currently selected piece is immobile, we should control the other piece to reach the goal (or free space).

CLICK likely changes which piece is active and/or swaps the two selectables’ positions. Since the currently selected piece cannot MOVE at all, we should CLICK the other selectable to work with the second piece and see if it has different mobility/reach toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to reposition/place the selected selectable onto the clicked pixel region if it’s a valid landing area; the goal may require clicking a point well inside its 5x5 bounds (not just grid-aligned). Previous goal clicks may have missed the valid landing/overlap check. Movement by arrows is irrelevant here due to walls.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is currently controlled (the 'player'); MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by collidable walls. Goal is to get all selectables onto goal tile(s).

Hypothesis: MOVE_* shifts the selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) but is blocked by adjacent walls. CLICK on the other selectable swaps the two pieces regardless of walls. Potential additional rule: CLICKing a wall cell may toggle/remove it (creating a corridor), which would explain why otherwise the level is impossible.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* is standard grid movement but fully walled-in here; CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece to the clicked pixel position if that destination is a valid/allowed tile (possibly goal tiles allow placement even if otherwise blocked). Earlier failed goal-adjacent clicks may have missed the exact required goal anchor pixel.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches the active/selected piece (the engine reports this as the 'player' changing position). MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) but may be blocked by adjacent walls. Need to verify whether the other selectable has any legal movement toward the goal despite the semantic grid suggesting both are boxed in.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable likely changes which piece is active (or swaps active control), which may change available movement options. Since the current selected piece is immobile, I should switch to the other selectable and check its movement options.

CLICK likely snaps to the 3-pixel grid (all successful clicks were multiples of 3) and can move/teleport the selected piece to a valid empty goal cell if the target is grid-aligned and not collidable. Goal object coords are not multiples of 3, so we must click a grid-aligned pixel inside the goal area (e.g., [36,18]).

CLICK appears to attempt to relocate the selected piece to the clicked cell (or a valid grid-aligned spot). Some clicks fail if not aligned to the underlying 3-pixel cell grid or if the destination is invalid/blocked. Goal tiles likely require landing on a specific grid-aligned cell within the goal region.

Actions (MOVE/CLICK) have been consuming steps, and at 0 remaining the engine likely prevents further progress. CLICK seems to attempt teleporting the selected piece to the clicked position but fails if the destination is blocked/invalid. With no remaining steps, only a last attempt that might still be accepted despite the step counter could possibly succeed.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked with `+`, with the extra constraint that if there are multiple `+` tiles they must all be occupied by pieces at the same time for the engine to report WIN; completing all levels wins the whole game within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A piece can be selected and then moved via actions; CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked grid coordinate, but the move only succeeds if the destination is reachable/valid under the level’s constraints. When the destination is blocked (by walls/obstacles or an invalid/unreachable target), the piece’s position does not change but the step is still consumed. The logs suggest movement is discrete on grid coordinates, obstacles prevent movement, and efficient step use is required because running out of steps ends the attempt.
